channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Defense secretary pulls Trump critic Gen. Milley's security clearance and protective detail
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pulling the security protections and clearance of retired Joint Chiefs chairman Gen. Mark Milley.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wisconsin mom urges Trump admin to launch 'pivotal' probe into alleged race-based discrimination against son
item.description: A Wisconsin mother has filed a complaint with the Department of Education over an incident where she says her son was discriminated against by his school because he is white.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked a push from President Donald Trump to pause federal funding while his administration conducts an across-the-board ideological review to uproot progressive initiatives. The order capped the most chaotic day for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: New results from a national exam find that America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doron Clark wins Senate special election, DFLers take back the Minnesota Senate
item.description: DFLer Doron Clark won in the deep-blue Minneapolis district with 90 percent of the vote, according to unofficial results.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Kern County leaders vote to make registrar of voters its own position
item.description: The county combined those positions with the clerk and registrar of voters back in 1995, due to budgeting issues. Those five roles have been connected ever since.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Six servicemembers challenge Trump's transgender military executive order
item.description: The Trump administration is being sued over a transgender military executive order, signed late Monday, by six military servicemembers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MAGA candidate wins GOP primary in Florida race to replace Matt Gaetz in Congress
item.description: The candidate endorsed by President Donald Trump won the Republican primary in a special congressional election to replace former U.S. Rep. Matt Gaetz in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uptick in ICE operations will soon strain resources: Ex-director
item.description: "Do we have the bed space? We're going to require a lot more resources and money to do that," said former ICE Director Patrick J. Lechleitner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Six active duty service members file first lawsuit challenging Trump's transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Six transgender active duty service members and two former service members who seek re-enlistment on Tuesday filed the first lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump’s executive order that calls for revising policy on transgender troops and probably sets the stage for banning the
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pence group uses Trump's criticism against RFK Jr. in new ad
item.description: An advocacy group founded by former Vice President Mike Pence is escalating its fight against Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: White House press briefing discusses federal assistance, deportation
item.description: Karoline Leavitt addressed the media from the White House's briefing room for the first time on Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Duffy nomination to run Transportation Department
item.description: Former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) moved a step closer to becoming the next Transportation secretary as the Senate voted unanimously to tee up a final vote to confirm him. Senators voted 97-0 to invoke cloture on Duffy’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order taking aim at DEI programs in the military
item.description: Trump said the diversity programs discourage merit and leadership, and discriminate on the basis of race and sex. The directive was among a series of orders that seeks to reshape the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: K-12 education and the Minnesota Legislature: 5 things to watch this session
item.description: Even as Minnesota lawmakers struggle to start their new session, education committee leaders in the House and Senate are quietly starting to set budget and policy priorities. Here’s a look at what might be coming.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Doctors opposing RFK Jr. rally in the lead-up to his confirmation
item.description: Many health professionals are lining up against Trump's pick for health secretary. They say his anti-vaccine views could cost lives. Some of his supporters embrace his stance.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: New Trump orders on transgender troops, COVID and more expected on Hegseth’s first day
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will sign a flurry of executive orders focused on the military, including to reinstate troops booted for refusing COVID-19 vaccines and remove “transgender ideology” from the force.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate tees up more confirmations as House GOP talks Trump agenda
item.description: (The Hill) — The Senate is set to churn through more of President Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees this week, with a handful of his controversial picks set to field questions during their confirmation hearings. Tulsi Gabbard, Trump’s pick to be director of national intelligence; Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff threat
item.description: The White House claimed victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S. on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump threatened various sanctions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Target gets booted from Twin Cities Pride festival after rolling back DEI efforts
item.description: Twin Cities Pride executive director Andi Otto says the nonprofit made the decision because Target is not standing up “against those who are fighting against us.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Churches have a long history of being safe havens
item.description: For centuries, houses of worship have served as havens for people needing refuge — and, in recent decades, sanctuary from the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more refugees to 'just clean out' Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday he'd like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting to "just clean out" the war-torn area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Early favorite emerges in Republican primary for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – A candidate has emerged as an early favorite in the Republican primary for New Jersey governor, a new Emerson College Polling/PIX11/The Hill poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC
